message:
feat(session): batched index updates, RawValue frames, and partial hydration tracking

Session index (session_index.rs):
- Batch index updates by sessions_root for amortized DB access: group
  pending updates, prepare all SessionMeta entries (IO-bound, no lock),
  then batch-upsert into SQLite in a single transaction per root.
- Extract file_stats() helper for mtime/size metadata collection.
- Type aliases PendingIndexKey/PendingIndexBatch for cleaner signatures.
- Improved retry logging with structured tracing fields.
- Background maintenance thread now handles cleanup_temp_files().

Session store v2 (session_store_v2.rs):
- Migrate SegmentFrame payload from serde_json::Value to Box<RawValue>
  to avoid re-serializing the payload when writing frames. The payload
  is serialized once to a string, then wrapped as RawValue and written
  directly, eliminating a redundant serialize→parse→serialize round-trip.
- Add MAX_FRAME_READ_BYTES (100MB) guard to prevent OOM on corrupted
  segment files during frame reading.
- append_entry() now uses seek-based atomic write pattern: seek to end,
  write buffer, verify post-write length matches expected offset, and
  truncate on partial write failure for crash resilience.
- Single write_all() call with pre-assembled buffer instead of separate
  payload + newline writes to reduce syscall count.
- Add #[allow(clippy::needless_pass_by_value)] where appropriate.

Session (session.rs):
- Add v2_message_count_offset field to track the delta between total
  manifest message count and in-memory loaded messages for partial/tail
  hydration scenarios.
- Session index lookup now spawns on background thread to avoid blocking
  the main thread on slow disk.
- open_from_v2_dir() tracks hydration offset and sets v2_partial_hydration
  / v2_resume_mode fields based on load mode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
